OPTIMIZATION 1: Check cache first (eliminates redundant LLM calls)\n261\t        cached_result = self._get_cached_classification(utterance, session_id)\n262\t        if cached_result is not None:\n263\t            # Return empty timing breakdown for cached results (no actual LLM call was made)\n264\t            empty_breakdown = {\n265\t                \"cached\": True,\n266\t                \"prompt_construction_ms\": 0,\n267\t                \"proxy_call_ms\": 0,\n268\t                \"proxy_network_ms\": 0,\n269\t                \"proxy_inference_ms\": 0,\n270\t                \"json_parse_ms\": 0,\n271\t                \"process_ms\": 0,\n272\t                \"total_ms\": 0,\n273\t                \"intents_count\": len(cached_result),\n274\t            }\n275\t            return cached_result, empty_breakdown\n276\t\n277\t        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n278\t        retry_count = 0\n279\t        raw_response = None\n280\t\n281\t        try:\n282\t            # Measure total classification time\n283\t            classify_start = time.perf_counter()\n284\t\n285\t            # Measure prompt construction time\n286\t            prompt_start = time.perf_counter()\n287\t\n288\t            # Use dedicated router client with 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n289\t            client = await self._get_router_zai_client()\n290\t\n291\t            # Build user message\n292\t            user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}\"\n293\t\n294\t            logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n295\t\n296\t            # Build system prompt from prompts/router.md\n297\t            # (hot-reload enabled via reload manager)\n298\t            system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n299\t\n300\t            prompt_ms = (time.perf_counter() - prompt_start) * 1000\n301\t\n302\t            # Measure ZAI proxy call time (network + model inference)\n303\t            # The LLM client now returns timing breakdown separating network latency from model inference\n304\t            proxy_start = time.perf_counter()\n305\t            response_data = await client.call_simple(\n306\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n307\t                user_message=user_message,\n308\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n309\t                max_tokens=128,  # Extract content and timing from response\n316\t            # response_data is now a dict with content and timing breakdown\n317\t            response = response_data.get(\"content\", \"\")\n318\t            timing_network_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_network_ms\")\n319\t            timing_inference_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_inference_ms\")\n320\t\n321\t            # Store raw response for error reporting\n322\t            raw_response = response\n323\t\n324\t            # Measure JSON parsing time\n325\t            parse_start = time.perf_counter()\n326\t            try:\n327\t                # Optimized JSON parsing using centralized parser\n328\t                # Uses single-pass find()/rfind() approach (faster than split/rsplit)\n329\t                # Handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced responses efficiently\n330\t                # See src/llm/response_parser.py for implementation\n331\t                intents_data = parse_llm_response(response, strip_fences=True, expect_json=True)\n332\t            except ParseLLMError as e:\n333\t                # Already has raw_response attached; just re-raise\n334\t                raise e\n335\t            parse_ms = (time.perf_counter() - parse_start) * 1000\n336\t\n337\t            # Measure classification processing time\n338\t            process_start = time.perf_counter()\n339\t            classifications = []\n340\t\n341\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n342\t                # Log detailed timing breakdown for latency profiling\n365\t            # This shows WHERE the 1,587-2,074ms latency is spent across the 4 investigation areas\n366\t            network_str = f\"{timing_network_ms:.2f}\" if timing_network_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n367\t            inference_str = f\"{timing_inference_ms:.2f}\" if timing_inference_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n368\t            logger.info(\n369\t                f\"router_timing breakdown: \"\n370\t                f\"prompt_construction_ms={prompt_ms:.2f} \"\n371\t                f\"proxy_call_ms={proxy_ms:.2f} \"\n372\t                f\"proxy_network_ms={network_str} \"\n373\t                f\"proxy_inference_ms={inference_str} \"\n374\t                f\"json_parse_ms={parse_ms:.2f} \"\n375\t                f\"process_ms={process_ms:.2f} \"\n376\t                f\"total_ms={total_ms:.2f} \"\n377\t                f\"intents={len(classifications)}\"\n378\t            )\n379\t\n380\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n381\t\n382\t            # Build timing breakdown for storage\n383\t            # Now includes separate network and inference timing measured by LLM client\n384\t            timing_breakdown = {\n385\t                \"prompt_construction_ms\": round(prompt_ms, 2),\n386\t                \"proxy_call_ms\": round(proxy_ms, 2),  # Total round-trip time (network + inference)\n387\t                \"proxy_network_ms\": round(timing_network_ms, 2) if timing_network_ms is not None else None,\n388\t                \"proxy_inference_ms\": round(timing_inference_ms, 2) if timing_inference_ms is not None else None,\n389\t                \"json_parse_ms\": round(parse_ms, 2),\n390\t                \"process_ms\": round(process_ms, 2),\n391\t                \"total_ms\": round(total_ms, 2),\n392\t                \"intents_count\": len(classifications),\n393\t            }\n394\t\n395\t            # OPTIMIZATION 1 (cont): Cache successful classifications\n396\t            self._cache_classification(utterance, session_id, classifications)\n397\t\n398\t            return classifications, timing_breakdown\n399\t\n400\t        except ParseLLMError as e:\n401\t            \"\"\"\n402\t            First parsing attempt failed - implement corrective retry.\n403\t\n404\t            Router uses corrective retry because it's the first step in the pipeline.\n405\t            A malformed router response cascades to everything downstream.\n406\t            One retry is justified because it's cheap (same prompt, 2048 tokens).\n407\t            \"\"\"\n408\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response: {e}\")\n409\t            raw_output = e.raw_response or raw_response  # Preserve for error reporting\n410\t\n411\t            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n412\t            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n413\t                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n414\t                retry_count += 1\n415\t                try:\n416\t                    # Retry once with same parameters\n417\t                    classifications, _ = await self.classify_utterance(\n418\t                        utterance=utterance,\n419\t                        session_id=session_id,\n420\t                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n421\t                    )\n422\t                    return classifications, _\n423\t                except ParseLLMError as retry_e:\n424\t                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n425\t                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n426\t                        parse_error=str(retry_e),\n427\t                        raw_output=retry_e.raw_response or raw_response,\n428\t                        retry_count=retry_count,\n429\t                    ) from retry_e\n430\t\n431\t            # No retry allowed or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n432\t            raise RouterMalformedError(\n433\t                parse_error=str(e),\n434\t                raw_output=raw_output,\n435\t                retry_count=retry_count,\n436\t            ) from e\n437\t\n438\t        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n439\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n440\t            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n441\t\n442\t        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n443\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n444\t            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n445\t\n446\t        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n447\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n448\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n449\t\n450\t        except Exception as e:\n451\t            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n452\t            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n453\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n454\t\n455\t    async def route_utterance(\n456\t        self,\n457\t        utterance: str,\n458\t        utterance_id: str,\n459\t        session_id: str,\n460\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n461\t        \"\"\"\n462\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n463\t\n464\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n465\t\n466\t        Args:\n467\t            utterance: The user utterance\n468\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n469\t            session_id: Session ID\n470\t\n471\t        Returns:\n472\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\n473\t\n474\t        Raises:\n475\t            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n476\t        \"\"\"\n477\t        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n478\t        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n479\t\n480\t        try:\n481\t            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n482\t            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n483\t            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n484\t            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n485\t            classifications, timing_breakdown = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n486\t            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n487\t\n488\t            # Store router timing breakdown in utterance record\n489\t            try:\n490\t                store = await self._get_store()\n491\t                await store.update_utterance_router_timing(utterance_id, timing_breakdown)\n492\t                logger.info(f\"Stored router timing breakdown for utterance {utterance_id[:8]}\")\n493\t            except Exception as e:\n494\t                logger.warning(f\"Failed to store router timing breakdown: {e}\")\n495\t                # Non-fatal: continue with routing\n496\t\n497\t            routed_intents = []\n498\t            for classification in classifications:\n499\t                # Create intent ID\n500\t                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n501\t\n502\t                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n503\t                    intent_id=intent_id,\n504\t                    classification=classification,\n505\t                    session_id=session_id,\n506\t                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n507\t                    router_ms=router_ms,\n508\t                )\n509\t                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n510\t\n511\t            return routed_intents\n512\t\n513\t        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n514\t            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n515\t            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n516\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n517\t            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n518\t                utterance=utterance,\n519\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n520\t                session_id=session_id,\n521\t                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n522\t                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n523\t                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n524\t            )\n525\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n526\t\n527\t        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n528\t            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n529\t            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n530\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n531\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n532\t                utterance=utterance,\n533\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n534\t                session_id=session_id,\n535\t                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n536\t            )\n537\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n538\t\n539\t        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n540\t            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n541\t            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n542\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n543\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n544\t                utterance=utterance,\n545\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n546\t                session_id=session_id,\n547\t                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n548\t            )\n549\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n550\t\n551\t        except RouterProxyError as e:\n552\t            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n553\t            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n554\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n555\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n556\t                utterance=utterance,\n557\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n558\t                session_id=session_id,\n559\t                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n560\t            )\n561\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n562\t\n563\t    async def process_intent(\n564\t        self,\n565\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n566\t    ) -> dict:\n567\t        \"\"\"\n568\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n569\t\n570\t        Args:\n571\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n572\t\n573\t        Returns:\n574\t            Result dictionary with status and data\n575\t        \"\"\"\n576\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n577\t\n578\t        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n579\t        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n580\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n581\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n582\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n583\t        # the dispatch itself.\n584\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n585\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n586\t\n587\t        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n588\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n589\t            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n590\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n591\t            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n592\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n593\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n594\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n595\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n596\t            )\n597\t\n598\t            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n599\t            store = await self._get_store()\n600\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n601\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n602\t                \"resolved\",\n603\t                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n604\t            )\n605\t\n606\t            return {\n607\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n608\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n609\t                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n610\t                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n611\t            }\n612\t\n613\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n614\t            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n615\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n616\t            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n617\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n618\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n619\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n620\t            )\n621\t\n622\t            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n623\t            store = await self._get_store()\n624\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n625\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n626\t                \"resolved\",\n627\t                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n628\t            )\n629\t\n630\t            return {\n631\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n632\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n633\t                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n634\t                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n635\t            }\n636\t\n637\t        try:\n638\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n639\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n640\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n641\t            else:\n642\t                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n643\t                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n644\t        except Exception:\n645\t            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n646\t            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n647\t            raise\n648\t\n649\t        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n650\t        return result\n651\t\n652\t    async def _persist_timings(\n653\t        self,\n654\t        intent_id: str,\n655\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n656\t    ) -> None:\n657\t        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n658\t        try:\n659\t            store = await self._get_store()\n660\t            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n661\t        except Exception as e:\n662\t            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n663\t\n664\t    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n665\t        self,\n666\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n667\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n668\t    ) -> dict:\n669\t        \"\"\"\n670\t        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n671\t\n672\t        Args:\n673\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n674\t            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n675\t                fetch_total_ms, and registry = get_registry()\n700\t            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n701\t                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n702\t                if location = entry.display_path\n707\t                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n708\t                else:\n709\t                    logger.info(f\"No )\n719\t\n720\t            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n721\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n722\t                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n723\t session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n727\t                    repo_path=repo_path,\n728\t                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n729\t                    host_alias=host_alias,\n730\t                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n731\t                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n732\t                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n733\t                ),\n734\t            )\n735\t\n736\t            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n737\t            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n738\t            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n739\t            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n740\t            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n741\t            total_sources = len(get_fetch_commands(fetch_intent_type))\n742\t            completed_sources:           # Track first source timing for fetch_first_source_ms metric\n747\t                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n748\t                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n749\t\n750\t                # Increment completed count\n751\t                completed_sources[0] += 1\n752\t\n753\t                # Broadcast progress event to canvas ('3/5 sources in')\n754\t                asyncio.create_task(broadcast_fetch_progress(\n755\t                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n756\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n757\t                    completed=completed_sources[0],\n758\t                    total=total_sources,\n759\t                    source_name=source.value,\n760\t                    source_status=result.status,\n761\t                ))\n762\t\n763\t            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_fetch_progress)\n764\t            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n765\t            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n766\t                timings.record(\n767\t                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n768\t                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n769\t                )\n770\t\n771\t            # Check for terminal failure: ALL sources failed (Degraded-State UX)\n772\t            if fetch_result.terminal_failure == \"all_sources_failed\":\n773\t                logger.error(\n774\t                    f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: \"\n775\t                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.failed)} failed, \"\n776\t                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.timed_out)} timed out\"\n777\t                )\n778\t\n779\t                # Build failed sources list for error event\n780\t                failed_sources = []\n781\t                for source, result in fetch_result.sources.items():\n782\t                    failed_sources.append({\n783\t                        \"source\": source.value,\n784\t                        \"status\": result.status,\n785\t                        \"error\": result.error,\n786\t                    })\n787\t\n788\t                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n789\t                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n790\t                await handler.broadcast_all_sources_failed(\n791\t                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n792\t                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n793\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n794\t                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n795\t                    failed_sources=failed_sources,\n796\t                )\n797\t\n798\t                # Update intent status to failed\n799\t                store = get_store()\n800\t                await store.update_intent_status(\n801\t                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n802\t                    \"failed\",\n803\t                    \"All fetch sources failed\",\n804\t                )\n805\t\n806\t                return {\n807\t                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n808\t                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n809\t                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n810\t                    \"error\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n811\t                    \"message\": \"No data — all required sources failed\",\n812\t                    \"terminal_failure\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n813\t                }\n814\t\n815\t            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n816\t            required_sources_failed = [\n817\t                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n818\t                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n819\t                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n820\t            ]\n821\t\n822\t            if sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n825\t\n826\t                # Handle terminal failure\n827\t                await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n828\t                    routed_intent=routed_intent,\n829\t                    failure_reason=failure_reason,\n830\t                    error_type=\"required_source_failure\",\n831\t                )\n832\t\n833\t                return {\n834\t                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n835\t                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n836\t                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n837\t                    \"error\": failure_reason,\n838\t                    \"message\": \"Required sources failed - cannot proceed\",\n839\t                }\n840\t\n841\t            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n842\t            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n843\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n844\t                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n845\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n846\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n847\t                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n848\t                urgency=classification.urgency,\n849\t            )\n850\t\n851\t            synth_start = timings.clock()\n852\t            try:\n853\t                synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n854\t            except Exception as synth_e:\n855\t                # ZAI failure at synthesize stage - broadcast degraded_raw_data\n856\t                # (Degraded-State UX: fetched data is never discarded)\n857\t                logger.error(\n858\t                    f\"Synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {synth_e}\"\n859\t                )\n860\t\n861\t                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n862\t                await handler.broadcast_degraded_raw_data(\n863\t                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n864\t                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n865\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n866\t                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n867\t                    fetched_context=fetch_result,\n868\t                    error_reason=str(synth_e),\n869\t                )\n870\t\n871\t                # Update intent status to failed\n872\t                store = get_store()\n873\t                await )\n878\t\n879\t                return {\n880\t                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n881\t                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n882\t                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n883\t                    \"error\": \"synthesize_failed\",\n884\t                    \"message\": \"Summary unavailable — showing raw fetch data\",\n885\t                    \"degraded\": True,\n886\t                }\n887\t\n888\t            timings.record(\"synthesize_total_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(synth_start))\n889\t            # synthesize_first_token_ms is not            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n894\t            store = get_store()\n895\t            _topic_type_map = {\n896\t                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n897\t                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n898\t            }\n899\t            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n900\t            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n901\t                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n902\t                topic_type=topic_type,\n903\t                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n904\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n905\t            )\n906\t            await lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n913\t            )\n914\t\n915\t            result_id = await store.create_result(\n916\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n917\t                topic_id=topic_id,\n918\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n919\t                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n920\t                data=synthesize_result.data,\n921\t        }\n954\t\n955\t        except Exception as e:\n956\t            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n957\t\n958\t            # Detect terminal FetchIntentType:\n1014\t        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\n1015\t\n1016\t        For lookup intents, route to the subtype-specific fetch matrix based on lookup_kind.\n1017\t        \"\"\"\n1018\t        # Map enum values by string\n1019\t        type_map = {\n1020\t            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n1021\t            IntentType.ACTION:           return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_DOCS\n1038\t\n1039\t        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n1040\t\n1041\t    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n1042\t entries - user intent doesn't change frequently in conversation\n_ROUTER_CACHE: dict[tuple[str, str], tuple[list, float]] = {}\n_CACHE_TTL = 900  # 15 minutes - longer TTL for better hit rate in conversation flow\n_MAX_CACHE_SIZE = 1000  # Increased capacity for more cache hits\n\n\n# Router error types for degraded-state handling\nclass RouterError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Base exception for router errors.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterTimeoutError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM call timed out.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterQuotaError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM call \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n    STATUS = \"status\"\n    ACTION = \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n user input\n    STUCK = \"stuck\"  # Stuck intent - task blocked by circuit breaker\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass IntentClassification:\n    \"\"\"Result of intent classification.\"\"\"\n    intent_type: low\n    lookup_kind: str | None = None  # lookup intents only: 'logs' | 'config' | 'docs'\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RoutedIntent:\n    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    classification: IntentClassification\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    # router_ms is measured once around classify_utterance() in route_utterance\n    # (one LLM call per segmentation prompt. Segment the utterance into \"\n    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n    \"intent objects.\"\n)\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n\n    Latency optimizations:\n    - LRU cache for repeated utterances (5-minute TTL)\n    - Reduced max_tokens (128 vs 256) for faster generation\n    - Dedicated 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n        self.store = store\n        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._router_zai_client = None  # Dedicated client with 10s timeout\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_router_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create dedicated ZAI client for router with optimized settings for low latency.\"\"\"\n        if self._router_zai_client is None:\n            from ..escalate.llm import get_router_zai_client\n            # Router needs fail-fast behavior with aggressive timeout\n            # 8-second timeout allows: ~2s connection + ~4-5s inference + ~1s parsing\n            self._router_zai_client = get_router_zai_client(timeout=8.0)\n        return self._router_zai_client\n\n    def _get_utterance_hash(self, utterance: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate hash key for utterance caching.\"\"\"\n        import hashlib\n        return hashlib.md5(utterance.encode()).hexdigest()\n\n    def _get_cached_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str) -> list[IntentClassification] | None:\n        \"\"\"Check cache for existing classification.\"\"\"\n        utterance_hash = self._get_utterance_hash(utterance)\n        cache_key = (utterance_hash, session_id)\n\n        if cache_key in _ROUTER_CACHE:\n            classifications, timestamp = _ROUTER_CACHE[cache_key]\n            age = time.time() - timestamp\n            if age < _CACHE_TTL:\n                logger.info(f\"Router cache HIT for utterance hash {utterance_hash[:8]} (age: {age:.1f}s)\")\n                return classifications\n            else:\n                # Remove expired entry\n                del _ROUTER_CACHE[cache_key]\n                logger.info(f\"Router cache EXPIRED for utterance hash {utterance_hash[:8]}\")\n\n        return None\n\n    def _cache_classification(self, utterance: str, session_id: str, classifications: list[IntentClassification]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Cache classification result.\"\"\"\n        # Prune cache if at capacity\n        if len(_ROUTER_CACHE) >= _MAX_CACHE_SIZE:\n            # Remove oldest entry (first in dict)\n            oldest_key = next(iter(_ROUTER_CACHE))\n            del _ROUTER_CACHE[oldest_key]\n            logger.info(f\"Router cache pruned oldest entry (size: {len(_ROUTER_CACHE)})\")\n\n        utterance_hash = self._get_utterance_hash(utterance)\n        cache_key = (utterance_hash, session_id)\n        _ROUTER_CACHE[cache_key] = (classifications, time.time())\n        logger.info(f\"Router cache STORED utterance hash {utterance_hash[:8]} (cache size: {len(_ROUTER_CACHE)})\")\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load the router segmentation prompt via hot-reload manager.\n\n        Uses the cached prompt from the hot-reload manager, which only reloads\n        when the file mtime changes. This avoids disk I/O on every classification.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"router\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt: {e}\")\n            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n\n    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the system prompt from the router segmentation prompt.\n\n        Latency optimization: Removed urgency prompt splicing from hot path.\n        The router still classifies urgency based on core intent patterns,\n        without the extra token overhead of separate urgency rules.\n        \"\"\"\n        return self._load_router_prompt()\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        retry_on_malformed: bool = True,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n            retry_on_malformed: If True, perform one corrective retry on JSON parse failure\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n\n        Raises:\n            RouterTimeoutError: LLM call timed out\n            RouterQuotaError: LLM quota exhausted\n            RouterProxyError: LLM proxy unreachable\n            RouterMalformedError: Malformed JSON after corrective retry\n        \"\"\"\n        # OPTIMIZATION 1: Check cache first (eliminates redundant LLM calls)\n        cached_result = self._get_cached_classification(utterance, session_id)\n        if cached_result is not None:\n            # Return empty timing breakdown for cached results (no actual LLM call was made)\n            empty_breakdown = {\n                \"cached\": True,\n                \"prompt_construction_ms\": 0,\n                \"proxy_call_ms\": 0,\n                \"proxy_network_ms\": 0,\n                \"proxy_inference_ms\": 0,\n                \"json_parse_ms\": 0,\n                \"process_ms\": 0,\n                \"total_ms\": 0,\n                \"intents_count\": len(cached_result),\n            }\n            return cached_result, empty_breakdown\n\n        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n        retry_count = 0\n        raw_response = None\n\n        try:\n            # Measure total classification time\n            classify_start = time.perf_counter()\n\n            # Measure prompt construction time\n            prompt_start = time.perf_counter()\n\n            # Use dedicated router client with 10s timeout for fail-fast behavior\n            client = await self._get_router_zai_client()\n\n            # Build user message\n            user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}\"\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n            # Build system prompt from prompts/router.md\n            # (hot-reload enabled via reload manager)\n            system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n\n            prompt_ms = (time.perf_counter() - prompt_start) * 1000\n\n            # Measure ZAI proxy call time (network + model inference)\n            # The LLM client now returns timing breakdown separating network latency from model inference\n            proxy_start = time.perf_counter()\n            response_data = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=128,  # OPTIMIZATION 2: Reduced from 256 to 128 - intent JSON is typically 50-80 tokens\n                temperature=0.0,\n                return_timing=True,  # Request timing breakdown from LLM client\n            )\n            proxy_ms = (time.perf_counter() - proxy_start) * 1000\n\n            # Extract content and timing from response\n            # response_data is now a dict with content and timing breakdown\n            response = response_data.get(\"content\", \"\")\n            timing_network_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_network_ms\")\n            timing_inference_ms = response_data.get(\"timing_inference_ms\")\n\n            # Store raw response for error reporting\n            raw_response = response\n\n            # Measure JSON parsing time\n            parse_start = time.perf_counter()\n            try:\n                # Optimized JSON parsing using centralized parser\n                # Uses single-pass find()/rfind() approach (faster than split/rsplit)\n                # Handles GLM-4.7's markdown-fenced responses efficiently\n                # See src/llm/response_parser.py for implementation\n                intents_data = parse_llm_response(response, strip_fences=True, expect_json=True)\n            except ParseLLMError as e:\n                # Already has raw_response attached; just re-raise\n                raise e\n            parse_ms = (time.perf_counter() - parse_start) * 1000\n\n            # Measure classification processing time\n            process_start = time.perf_counter()\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n            process_ms = (time.perf_counter() - process_start) * 1000\n\n            total_ms = (time.perf_counter() - classify_start) * 1000\n\n            # Log detailed timing breakdown for latency profiling\n            # This shows WHERE the 1,587-2,074ms latency is spent across the 4 investigation areas\n            network_str = f\"{timing_network_ms:.2f}\" if timing_network_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n            inference_str = f\"{timing_inference_ms:.2f}\" if timing_inference_ms is not None else \"N/A\"\n            logger.info(\n                f\"router_timing breakdown: \"\n                f\"prompt_construction_ms={prompt_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"proxy_call_ms={proxy_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"proxy_network_ms={network_str} \"\n                f\"proxy_inference_ms={inference_str} \"\n                f\"json_parse_ms={parse_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"process_ms={process_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"total_ms={total_ms:.2f} \"\n                f\"intents={len(classifications)}\"\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n\n            # Build timing breakdown for storage\n            # Now includes separate network and inference timing measured by LLM client\n            timing_breakdown = {\n                \"prompt_construction_ms\": round(prompt_ms, 2),\n                \"proxy_call_ms\": round(proxy_ms, 2),  # Total round-trip time (network + inference)\n                \"proxy_network_ms\": round(timing_network_ms, 2) if timing_network_ms is not None else None,\n                \"proxy_inference_ms\": round(timing_inference_ms, 2) if timing_inference_ms is not None else None,\n                \"json_parse_ms\": round(parse_ms, 2),\n                \"process_ms\": round(process_ms, 2),\n                \"total_ms\": round(total_ms, 2),\n                \"intents_count\": len(classifications),\n            }\n\n            # OPTIMIZATION 1 (cont): Cache successful classifications\n            self._cache_classification(utterance, session_id, classifications)\n\n            return classifications, timing_breakdown\n\n        except ParseLLMError as e:\n            \"\"\"\n            First parsing attempt failed - implement corrective retry.\n\n            Router uses corrective retry because it's the first step in the pipeline.\n            A malformed router response cascades to everything downstream.\n            One retry is justified because it's cheap (same prompt, 2048 tokens).\n            \"\"\"\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response: {e}\")\n            raw_output = e.raw_response or raw_response  # Preserve for error reporting\n\n            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n                retry_count += 1\n                try:\n                    # Retry once with same parameters\n                    classifications, _ = await self.classify_utterance(\n                        utterance=utterance,\n                        session_id=session_id,\n                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n                    )\n                    return classifications, _\n                except ParseLLMError as retry_e:\n                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n                        parse_error=str(retry_e),\n                        raw_output=retry_e.raw_response or raw_response,\n                        retry_count=retry_count,\n                    ) from retry_e\n\n            # No retry allowed or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n            raise RouterMalformedError(\n                parse_error=str(e),\n                raw_output=raw_output,\n                retry_count=retry_count,\n            ) from e\n\n        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n\n        Raises:\n            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n        try:\n            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n            classifications, timing_breakdown = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n\n            # Store router timing breakdown in utterance record\n            try:\n                store = await self._get_store()\n                await store.update_utterance_router_timing(utterance_id, timing_breakdown)\n                logger.info(f\"Stored router timing breakdown for utterance {utterance_id[:8]}\")\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.warning(f\"Failed to store router timing breakdown: {e}\")\n                # Non-fatal: continue with routing\n\n            routed_intents = []\n            for classification in classifications:\n                # Create intent ID\n                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                    intent_id=intent_id,\n                    classification=classification,\n                    session_id=session_id,\n                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n                    router_ms=router_ms,\n                )\n                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n            return routed_intents\n\n        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterProxyError as e:\n            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n        # the dispatch itself.\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            )\n\n            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"resolved\",\n                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n            }\n\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"resolved\",\n                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n            }\n\n        try:\n            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n            else:\n                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n        except Exception:\n            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n            raise\n\n        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n        return result\n\n    async def _persist_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(\n                classification.intent_type,\n                classification.lookup_kind\n            )\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            from ..registry import get_project\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n            project_cfg = (\n                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n                if classification.project_slug else None\n            )\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                context=FetchContext(\n                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    repo_path=repo_path,\n                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n                    host_alias=host_alias,\n                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n            total_sources = len(get_fetch_commands(fetch_intent_type))\n            completed_sources: list[int] = [0]\n\n            def _on_fetch_progress(source, result) -> None:\n                \"\"\"Track fetch timing and broadcast progress events to pending card.\"\"\"\n                # Track first source timing for fetch_first_source_ms metric\n                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n\n                # Increment completed count\n                completed_sources[0] += 1\n\n                # Broadcast progress event to canvas ('3/5 sources in')\n                asyncio.create_task(broadcast_fetch_progress(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    completed=completed_sources[0],\n                    total=total_sources,\n                    source_name=source.value,\n                    source_status=result.status,\n                ))\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_fetch_progress)\n            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n                timings.record(\n                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n                )\n\n            # Check for terminal failure: ALL sources failed (Degraded-State UX)\n            if fetch_result.terminal_failure == \"all_sources_failed\":\n                logger.error(\n                    f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: \"\n                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.failed)} failed, \"\n                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.timed_out)} timed out\"\n                )\n\n                # Build failed sources list for error event\n                failed_sources = []\n                for source, result in fetch_result.sources.items():\n                    failed_sources.append({\n                        \"source\": source.value,\n                        \"status\": result.status,\n                        \"error\": result.error,\n                    })\n\n                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n                await handler.broadcast_all_sources_failed(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                    failed_sources=failed_sources,\n                )\n\n                # Update intent status to failed\n                store = get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"failed\",\n                    \"All fetch sources failed\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n                    \"message\": \"No data — all required sources failed\",\n                    \"terminal_failure\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n                }\n\n            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n            required_sources_failed = [\n                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n            ]\n\n            if required_sources_failed:\n                failure_reason = f\"Required data sources failed: {', '.join(s.value for s in required_sources_failed)}\"\n                logger.error(f\"Required sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n\n                # Handle terminal failure\n                await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                    routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                    failure_reason=failure_reason,\n                    error_type=\"required_source_failure\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": failure_reason,\n                    \"message\": \"Required sources failed - cannot proceed\",\n                }\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                urgency=classification.urgency,\n            )\n\n            synth_start = timings.clock()\n            try:\n                synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n            except Exception as synth_e:\n                # ZAI failure at synthesize stage - broadcast degraded_raw_data\n                # (Degraded-State UX: fetched data is never discarded)\n                logger.error(\n                    f\"Synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {synth_e}\"\n                )\n\n                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n                await handler.broadcast_degraded_raw_data(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                    fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                    error_reason=str(synth_e),\n                )\n\n                # Update intent status to failed\n                store = get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"failed\",\n                    f\"Synthesize failed: {synth_e}\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": \"synthesize_failed\",\n                    \"message\": \"Summary unavailable — showing raw fetch data\",\n                    \"degraded\": True,\n                }\n\n            timings.record(\"synthesize_total_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(synth_start))\n            # synthesize_first_token_ms is not measurable on the current\n            # call_simple path (no token stream) and is left NULL until the\n            # synthesize strand streams — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n\n            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n            store = get_store()\n            _topic_type_map = {\n                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n            }\n            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Derive result_type from intent classification (includes lookup_kind for lookups)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                data=synthesize_result.data,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=synthesize_result.data,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag so client knows which path to take\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"resolved\",\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"data\": synthesize_result.data,\n                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                \"coverage\": synthesize_result.coverage,\n                \"caveats\": synthesize_result.caveats,\n                \"card_fallback\": render_outcome.card_fallback,\n                \"rendered_html\": render_outcome.rendered_html,\n                \"component_id\": render_outcome.component_id,\n                \"message\": \"Intent synthesized successfully\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n            # Detect terminal failure and handle appropriately\n            await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                failure_reason=str(e),\n                error_type=\"worker_crash\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Fetch/synthesize failed - terminal error\",\n            }\n\n    async def _handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        failure_reason: str,\n        error_type: str,\n        bead_ref: str | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Handle terminal failure for a routed intent.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The intent that failed\n            failure_reason: Human-readable failure reason\n            error_type: Type of error (worker_crash, invalid_input, required_source_failure)\n            bead_ref: Associated bead reference (if applicable)\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..escalate.handler import handle_terminal_failure\n\n        # Get or create topic for failed card\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Failed: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                topic_type=\"exception\",\n            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to create topic for failed card: {e}\")\n            topic_id = None\n\n        # Handle terminal failure (updates intent status, stores in bead_watch, broadcasts SSE)\n        await handle_terminal_failure(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            failure_reason=failure_reason,\n            error_type=error_type,\n            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n        )\n\n    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType, lookup_kind: str | None = None) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\n\n        For lookup intents, route to the subtype-specific fetch matrix based on lookup_kind.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n            IntentType.STUCK: FetchIntentType.STUCK,\n        }\n\n        # For lookup intents with lookup_kind, route to the subtype-specific matrix\n        if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP and lookup_kind:\n            if lookup_kind == \"logs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_LOGS\n            elif lookup_kind == \"config\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_CONFIG\n            elif lookup_kind == \"docs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_DOCS\n\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n    ) -> dict | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n\n        Returns:\n            Fence context dict with bead_id, refusal_reason, refusal_count, fenced_at\n            if fenced, None return None\n\n            # Check if bead is fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at)\n            last_refusal_reason = bead_watch.get(\"last_refusal_reason\")\n            fenced_at = bead_watch.get(\"fenced_at\")\n            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n                return {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": last_refusal_reason or \"Fenced (no reason provided)\",\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": fenced_at,\n                }\n\n            return None\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking fence status for bead {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n            return None\n\n    async def _create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        fence_context: dict,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a stuck card when a fenced bead is detected during intent routing.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent\n            fence_context: Fence context from _check_fence_for_bead\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with stuck card details\n        \"\"\"\n        from datetime import datetime\n\n        bead_id = fence_context[\"bead_id\"]\n        refusal_reason = fence_context[\"refusal_reason\"]\n        refusal_count = fence_context[\"refusal_count\"]\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Creating stuck card for fenced bead {bead_id} \"\n            f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n\n            # Update intent type to 'stuck' and status to 'stuck'\n            await store.update_intent_type_and_status(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=\"stuck\",\n                status=\"stuck\",\n            )\n\n            # Get or create topic for this stuck card\n            topic_type = \"project\" if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else \"research\"\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Fenced: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[routed_intent.classification.project_slug] if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Link intent to topic\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Create stuck result\n            summary = f\"Task stuck — needs your input\"\n            data = {\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": f\"This task has been blocked after {refusal_count} refusals.\",\n                \"action_hint\": \"Review the bead and provide the missing information or context needed to proceed.\",\n                \"fence_detected_during\": \"intent_routing\",\n            }\n\n            # Derive result_type from original intent classification (not \"stuck\" state, includes lookup_kind)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=routed_intent.classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=routed_intent.classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=routed_intent.classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=summary,\n                data=data,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render stuck card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=data,\n                summary=summary,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created stuck card {result_id} for fenced bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            # Broadcast task_stuck event via SSE\n            broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n            await broadcaster.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=EventType.TASK_STUCK,\n                    data={\n                        \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n \"session_id\": routed_intent.session_id,\n                        \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                        \"timestamp\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n                    },\n                    target_session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                )\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": \"stuck\",\n                \"status\": \"stuck\",\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n }\n\n    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate a task-profile intent to a NEEDLESS bead.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to escalate\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records escalate_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with pending card or stuck card if fence detected\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Step 1: Check for existing fenced beads in the session before escalating\n        # This detects fence events when last_refusal_reason is set on existing beads\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            fenced_beads = self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                    routed_intent, fence_context\n                )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking for fenced beads: {e}\")\n            # Continue to escalation if fence check fails\n\n        # Step 2: No fenced beads detected - proceed with escalation\n        # Build escalate request\n        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n            intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            context={\n                \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n            },\n            metadata={\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n                \"confidence\": classification.confidence,\n            },\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Escalate to bead — escalate_ms budgets formulation + validation +\n            # bf create (task-profile dispatches only; NULL for hot-path rows).\n            esc_start = timings.clock()\n            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n            timings.record(\"escalate_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(esc_start))\n\n            # Check if the created bead is immediately fenced (race condition:\n            # watcher may have fenced it between escalate request and now)\n            if result.bead_id:\n                fence_context = self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                        routed_intent, fence_context\n                    )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"escalated\",\n                \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n                \"pending_card\": {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n
